---
title: "PQAR Statistical Analysis"
author: "Rafaela Drake"
date: "2026-06-12"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r}
#loading data (MIDUS Study)
getwd()
midus_data <-readRDS("~/PQAR 2026/Research Project/midus_cancer_aging_analysis_dataset.rds")
View(midus_data)
dim(midus_data)

colnames(midus_data)

#Install and load packages: tidyverse, gtsummary, flextable, broom, officer, mice, corrr, tibble, misty
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(flextable)
library(broom)
library(officer)
library(mice)
library(corrr)
library(tibble)
library(misty)
library(Hmisc) #corr
library(modelsummary) #overall regression table
library(naniar)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)



#package used for Latex (knitting to PDF for Mac)
library(tinytex)

#regression output plots
library(patchwork)

#data from each column (variable)
univariate_stats <- midus_data %>% select(grimage2:efficacy_STD)
#adds label of what summary value is produced (dependent on data type)
uni_labs <- list(
  grimage2        ~ "GrimAge2 (Epigenetic Age), mean (SD)",
  dunedinpace     ~ "DunedinPACE (Pace of Aging), mean (SD)",
  bmi             ~ "Body Mass Index (BMI), mean (SD)",
  age             ~ "Chronological Age (years), mean (SD)",
  sex             ~ "Sex, No. (%)",
  race_eth        ~ "Race/Ethnicity, No. (%)",
  marital         ~ "Marital Status, No. (%)",
  education       ~ "Educational Attainment, No. (%)",
  income          ~ "Annual Household Income, mean (SD)",
  num_chron       ~ "Number of Chronic Conditions, mean (SD)",
  smoking         ~ "Smoking Status, No. (%)",
  alcohol         ~ "Alcohol Consumption, No. (%)",
  famcancer       ~ "Family History of Cancer, No. (%)",
  cancer_history  ~ "Personal Cancer History, mean (SD)",
  cancerrisk      ~ "Perceived Cancer Risk, mean (SD)",
  efficacy        ~ "Cancer Prevention Self-Efficacy, mean (SD)",
  grimage2_STD    ~ "GrimAge2 (Standardized), mean (SD)",
  dunedinpace_STD ~ "DunedinPACE (Standardized), mean (SD)",
  cancerrisk_STD  ~ "Perceived Cancer Risk (Standardized), mean (SD)",
  efficacy_STD    ~ "Cancer Prevention Self-Efficacy (Standardized), mean (SD)"
)
#tblsummary() function to calculate values and make them into a table, digits = 3
univariate_stats_table <- univariate_stats %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.factor), ~ fct_explicit_na(., "Missing"))) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
      all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"
    ),
    missing_text = "Missing",
    digits = all_continuous() ~ 1,
    label = uni_labs
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("Table I. Univariate Statistics on MIDUS II Data")
#view table
univariate_stats_table

univariate_stats_table %>%
  as_gt() %>%
  gt::gtsave("univariate_stats_table.docx")
dim(midus_data)

# - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - CORRELATION MATRIX - - - - - - -

#ADD
#include p-values (add.p)
#function add overall within table 

#Table 4: Zero-Order Correlation Matrix -> perceived cancer risk unadjusted relationships between variables with #emphasis on outcomes and exposure
cor_vars <- midus_data %>%
  select(grimage2, dunedinpace, age, num_chron, cancerrisk_STD, efficacy)

#Convert to a matrix
vars_matrix <- as.matrix(cor_vars)

#Correlation matrix using Hmisc
correlation_results <- Hmisc::rcorr(vars_matrix, type=c("pearson"))
#extract correlation matrix
r_matrix <- correlation_results$r
#extract p-values
p_matrix <- correlation_results$P

#adding starts for p-values and keep lower triagnle
r_formatted <- format(round(r_matrix, 3), nsmall = 3) #issue
stars <- ifelse(p_matrix <0.001, "***",
                ifelse(p_matrix < 0.01, "**",
                       ifelse(p_matrix <0.05, "*", "")))
#combined
final_cor_matrix <- matrix(paste0(r_formatted, stars), nrow = nrow(r_matrix))
rownames(final_cor_matrix) <- rownames(r_matrix)
colnames(final_cor_matrix) <- colnames(r_matrix)

#remove self-correlations
diag(final_cor_matrix) <- ""

#only keep lower triangle
final_cor_matrix[upper.tri(final_cor_matrix)] <- ""

#convert to df and format row names as a separate column
final_df <- as.data.frame(final_cor_matrix)
final_df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(final_df, var = "Variable")

#Professional variable labels
#vector mapping old names to new labels
clean_labels <- c(
  "grimage2"        = "GrimAge2",
  "dunedinpace"     = "DunedinPACE",
  "age"             = "Age",
  "num_chron"       = "Number of chronic conditions",
  "cancerrisk_STD"  = "Perceived cancer risk z-score",
  "efficacy"        = "Self-efficacy"
)

#rename in final df
final_df$Variable <- clean_labels[final_df$Variable]
#add numbers to row names
final_df$Variable <- paste0(1:nrow(final_df), ". ", final_df$Variable) 
#change column headers to numbers (corresponding with row numbering)
colnames(final_df)[2:ncol(final_df)] <- 1:(ncol(final_df) - 1)


#formatting (flextable)
ft <- flextable(final_df) %>%
  #set header labels
  set_header_labels(
    Variable = "Variable",
    grimage2 = "1",
    dunedinpace = "2",
    age = "3",
    num_chron = "4",
    cancerrisk_STD = "5",
    efficacy = "6") %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>% #APA
  bold(part = "header") %>%
  autofit() %>%
  fit_to_width(max_width = 6.5, unit = "in") %>%
  align(j = 1, align = "left", part = "all") %>% 
  align(j = 2:ncol(final_df), align = "center", part = "all") %>%
  # Add a table title
  add_header_lines("1.1. Table 3. Zero-order correlations among epigenetic aging measures, perceived cancer risk, age, self-efficacy, and chronic conditions.") %>%
  #footer with stars info
  add_footer_lines("* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001") %>%
  font(fontname = "Arial", part = "all")

ft

#export
save_as_docx(ft, path = "Table_4_Correlation_Matrix.docx")


# - - - - - - - - - - -NEXT- - - -- - - - - - 
#multiple imputation
# Conducting multiple imputation 

# Subsetting the data to include the two standardized outcomes, 
# the standardized exposure, the moderator and the 11 covariates
#15 vars total 
# checking for row containing NA value in grimAge2_STD
print(is.na(midus_data$grimage2_STD))

midus_vars <- midus_data %>% 
              select("income", "famcancer", "efficacy",
                     "marital", "education", "grimage2_STD", "dunedinpace_STD",
                     "cancerrisk_STD", "race_eth", "age", "sex",
                     "smoking", "alcohol", "bmi") %>%
  # Removing row with NA value for grimage2_STD
  filter(
    !is.na(grimage2_STD) #dropping missing value from grimage2
  ) %>%
  # Categorical variables should be coded as factors in order to invoke polyreg
  mutate(
      race_eth = factor(race_eth),
      alcohol = factor(alcohol),
      sex = factor(sex),
      education = factor(education, ordered = TRUE),
      smoking = factor(smoking),
      marital = factor(marital),
      famcancer = factor(famcancer)
    )


# confirming NA value has been removed
print(is.na(midus_vars$grimage2_STD))

# Run Little's MCAR
mcar_test(midus_vars)
# statistic df  p-value  missing.patterns
# 212.3461	162	0.004844206	13	

# Create a clean character vector tracking all variables in our target analysis subset
analysis_variables <- names(midus_vars)

md.pattern(midus_data) # Counting number of NA
# Pattern summary: 1087 rows of 1156 that are complete. 

md.pairs(midus_data) # Determining four missing patterns 
# Looking at mr / rm values helps understand which values have overlapping data
# to make them eligible for calculating imputations. 

#missing summary function 
missing_summary <- function(data, vars) {
  data %>%
    summarise(
      across(
        all_of(vars), #across all the given vars
        ~ mean(is.na(.x)) * 100 # percentage of variables w/ missing values
      )
    ) %>% #create a data set w/ names - variable names, values - percent missing
    pivot_longer(
      cols = everything(),
      names_to = "variable",
      values_to = "percent_missing"
    ) %>%
  arrange(desc(percent_missing)) #arrange output in descending over 
}


missing_summary(midus_data, analysis_variables)

#predictor matrix 
pred <- mice(midus_vars, maxit = 0)$predictorMatrix 

# Creating a methods object
midus_methods <- make.method(midus_vars) 
midus_methods # Looking to check method types for categorical vars

# Assigning new methods to categorical variables
midus_methods["education"] <- "polr" # changed this from "polyreg" as education is an ordered categorical variable
#polyreg used for categorical values with 2+ categories
midus_methods["famcancer"] <- "logreg" #logreg for nominal categorical values
midus_methods["marital"] <- "polyreg"
midus_methods # Checking to ensure variables are updated

imp <- mice(
  midus_vars, # data: what we want to impute
  m = 10, # number of imputations to perform
  seed = 0864,
  printFlag = FALSE,
  method = midus_methods
)

# Extract the first completed imputed dataset
df_mice_completed <- complete(imp, action = 1)
df_mice_completed


# Re-run missing summary on the complete dataset to verify all missingness dropped to 0%
missing_summary(df_mice_completed, analysis_variables)

```


```{r regression}

#Regression Table

#grimage2 regressions
m1_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD 
                   + age + sex + race_eth + marital,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m2_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD 
                   + age + sex + race_eth + marital + education + income,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m3_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD 
                   + age + sex + race_eth + marital + education + income + famcancer,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m4_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD 
                   + age + sex + race_eth + marital + education + income + famcancer + efficacy,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m5_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD 
                   + age + sex + race_eth + marital + education + income + famcancer + efficacy + smoking + alcohol + bmi,
                      data = df_mice_completed)
#race_eth interaction
m6_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth 
                   + age + sex + marital + education + income + famcancer + efficacy + smoking + alcohol + bmi,
                      data = df_mice_completed)
m6_grim


#regression of dunedinpace_STD
m1_dune <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD 
                   + age + sex + race_eth + marital,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m2_dune <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD 
                   + age + sex + race_eth + marital + education + income,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m3_dune <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD 
                   + age + sex + race_eth + marital + education + income + famcancer,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m4_dune <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD 
                   + age + sex + race_eth + marital + education + income + famcancer + efficacy,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m5_dune <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD 
                   + age + sex + race_eth + marital + education + income + famcancer + efficacy + smoking + alcohol + bmi,
                      data = df_mice_completed)
#race_eth interaction
m6_dune <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth 
                   + age + sex + marital + education + income + famcancer + efficacy + smoking + alcohol + bmi,
                      data = df_mice_completed)
m6_dune


#Overall Models Data Frame:
grim_models <- list("M1" = m1_grim, "M2" = m2_grim, "M3" = m3_grim, 
                    "M4" = m4_grim, "M5" = m5_grim, "M6" = m6_grim)

dune_models <- list("M1" = m1_dune, "M2" = m2_dune, "M3" = m3_dune, 
                    "M4" = m4_dune, "M5" = m5_dune, "M6" = m6_dune)

format_with_broom <- function(model_list, outcome_label) {
  
  #terms for interaction
  interaction_terms <- c(
    "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethnon-Hispanic Black",
    "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethHispanic",
    "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethnon-Hispanic Other"
  )
  
  model_list %>%
    map_dfr(~ broom::tidy(.x, conf.int = TRUE), .id = "Model") %>%
    filter(term %in% c("cancerrisk_STD", interaction_terms)) %>%
    mutate(
      Beta    = sprintf("%.3f", estimate),
      CI      = paste0("[", sprintf("%.2f", conf.low), ", ", sprintf("%.2f", conf.high), "]"),
      pval    = if_else(p.value < 0.001, "p <0.001",
                        paste0("p = ", sprintf("%.3f", p.value)),
                        missing = NA_character_),
      Outcome   = outcome_label,
      Predictor = case_match(
        term,
        "cancerrisk_STD"                              ~ "Perceived Cancer Risk (Std.)",
        "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethnon-Hispanic Black"   ~ "  × Non-Hispanic Black",
        "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethHispanic"             ~ "  × Hispanic",
        "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethnon-Hispanic Other"   ~ "  × Non-Hispanic Other",
        .default = term
      ),
      # Stack β, CI, p vertically inside each cell
      Cell_Content = paste(Beta, CI, pval, sep = "\n")
    ) %>%
    select(Outcome, Predictor, Model, Cell_Content)
}

#bind rows and create reference row for reference group in interaction
raw_df <- bind_rows(
  format_with_broom(grim_models, "GrimAge2 Acceleration"),
  format_with_broom(dune_models, "DunedinPACE Pace of Aging")
) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Model, values_from = Cell_Content)

# Insert the Non-Hispanic White reference row (ref category, no coefficient)
# It should appear immediately before the 3 interaction contrast rows
ref_rows <- tibble(
  Outcome   = c("GrimAge2 Acceleration", "DunedinPACE Pace of Aging"),
  Predictor = "  × Non-Hispanic White (ref.)",
  M1 = "", M2 = "", M3 = "", M4 = "", M5 = "", M6 = ""
)

# Bind and sort so ref row sits above the contrasts, below main effect
manuscript_df <- bind_rows(raw_df, ref_rows) %>%
  mutate(
    Predictor = factor(Predictor, levels = c(
      "Perceived Cancer Risk (Std.)",
      "  × Non-Hispanic White (ref.)",
      "  × Non-Hispanic Black",
      "  × Hispanic",
      "  × Non-Hispanic Other"
    ))
  ) %>%
  arrange(Outcome, Predictor) %>%
  mutate(Predictor = as.character(Predictor))

##flextable

# Each outcome now has 5 rows: 1 main effect + 4 interaction rows
# Midpoint between the two clocks is after row 5

ft <- flextable(manuscript_df) %>%

  set_header_labels(
    Outcome   = "Biological Aging Clock",
    Predictor = "Predictor Variable",
    M1 = "Model 1", M2 = "Model 2", M3 = "Model 3",
    M4 = "Model 4", M5 = "Model 5", M6 = "Model 6"
  ) %>%

  align(j = c("M1","M2","M3","M4","M5","M6"), align = "center", part = "all") %>%
  align(j = c("Outcome","Predictor"),          align = "left",   part = "all") %>%

  # Merge the Outcome label across its 5 rows
  merge_v(j = "Outcome") %>%
  valign(j = "Outcome", valign = "top") %>%

  font(fontname = "Times New Roman", part = "all") %>%
  fontsize(size = 11, part = "all") %>%
  bold(part = "header") %>%

  #borders
  border_remove() %>%
  hline_top(border    = fp_border(color = "black", width = 1.5), part = "header") %>%
  hline_bottom(border = fp_border(color = "black", width = 1.5), part = "header") %>%
  hline_bottom(border = fp_border(color = "black", width = 1.5), part = "body") %>%

  # Divider between the two clocks (after row 5)
  hline(i = 5, border = fp_border(color = "gray", width = 0.5), part = "body") %>%

  # M1–M5 have no interaction terms
  colformat_char(j = c("M1","M2","M3","M4","M5"), na_str = "") %>%

  valign(valign = "top", part = "body") %>%
  autofit() %>%
  set_caption(caption = "Table 4. Multi-Model Regressions for Perceived Cancer Risk Predicting Epigenetic Age Acceleration.")

#formatting (condense)
ft <- ft %>%
  # Constrain model columns to a fixed narrow width
  width(j = c("M1","M2","M3","M4","M5","M6"), width = 0.8) %>%
  # Constrain descriptor columns
  width(j = "Outcome",    width = 1.0) %>%
  width(j = "Predictor",  width = 1.4)

ft
#export to word
read_docx() %>% 
  body_add_flextable(ft) %>% 
  print(target = "Output/rafaela_overall_regressions_table.docx")

# ==============================================================================
#Repeated but models 1-5 now include the race/ethnicity interaction term 


m1_grim_i <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth
                   + age + sex + marital,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m2_grim_i <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth
                   + age + sex + marital + education + income,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m3_grim_i <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth
                   + age + sex + marital + education + income + famcancer,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m4_grim_i <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth
                   + age + sex + marital + education + income + famcancer + efficacy,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m5_grim_i <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth
                   + age + sex + marital + education + income + famcancer + efficacy + smoking + alcohol + bmi,
                      data = df_mice_completed)



#regression of dunedinpace_STD
m1_dune_i <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth
                   + age + sex + marital,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m2_dune_i <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth
                   + age + sex + marital + education + income,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m3_dune_i <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth
                   + age + sex + marital + education + income + famcancer,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m4_dune_i <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth
                   + age + sex + marital + education + income + famcancer + efficacy,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

m5_dune_i <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD * race_eth
                   + age + sex + marital + education + income + famcancer + efficacy + smoking + alcohol + bmi,
                      data = df_mice_completed)

#Overall Models (1-5 with interaction) Data Frame:
grim_models <- list("M1" = m1_grim_i, "M2" = m2_grim_i, "M3" = m3_grim_i, 
                    "M4" = m4_grim_i, "M5" = m5_grim_i)

dune_models <- list("M1" = m1_dune_i, "M2" = m2_dune_i, "M3" = m3_dune_i, 
                    "M4" = m4_dune_i, "M5" = m5_dune_i)

format_with_broom <- function(model_list, outcome_label) {
  
  #terms for interaction
  interaction_terms <- c(
    "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethnon-Hispanic Black",
    "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethHispanic",
    "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethnon-Hispanic Other"
  )
  
  model_list %>%
    map_dfr(~ broom::tidy(.x, conf.int = TRUE), .id = "Model") %>%
    filter(term %in% c("cancerrisk_STD", interaction_terms)) %>%
    mutate(
      Beta    = sprintf("%.3f", estimate),
      CI      = paste0("[", sprintf("%.2f", conf.low), ", ", sprintf("%.2f", conf.high), "]"),
      pval    = if_else(p.value < 0.001, "p <0.001",
                        paste0("p = ", sprintf("%.3f", p.value)),
                        missing = NA_character_),
      Outcome   = outcome_label,
      Predictor = case_match(
        term,
        "cancerrisk_STD"                              ~ "Perceived Cancer Risk (Std.)",
        "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethnon-Hispanic Black"   ~ "  × Non-Hispanic Black",
        "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethHispanic"             ~ "  × Hispanic",
        "cancerrisk_STD:race_ethnon-Hispanic Other"   ~ "  × Non-Hispanic Other",
        .default = term
      ),
      # Stack β, CI, p vertically inside each cell
      Cell_Content = paste(Beta, CI, pval, sep = "\n")
    ) %>%
    select(Outcome, Predictor, Model, Cell_Content)
}

#bind rows and create reference row for reference group in interaction
raw_df <- bind_rows(
  format_with_broom(grim_models, "GrimAge2 Acceleration"),
  format_with_broom(dune_models, "DunedinPACE Pace of Aging")
) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Model, values_from = Cell_Content)

# Insert the Non-Hispanic White reference row (ref category, no coefficient)
# It should appear immediately before the 3 interaction contrast rows
ref_rows <- tibble(
  Outcome   = c("GrimAge2 Acceleration", "DunedinPACE Pace of Aging"),
  Predictor = "  × Non-Hispanic White (ref.)",
  M1 = "", M2 = "", M3 = "", M4 = "", M5 = ""
)

# Bind and sort so ref row sits above the contrasts, below main effect
manuscript_df <- bind_rows(raw_df, ref_rows) %>%
  mutate(
    Predictor = factor(Predictor, levels = c(
      "Perceived Cancer Risk (Std.)",
      "  × Non-Hispanic White (ref.)",
      "  × Non-Hispanic Black",
      "  × Hispanic",
      "  × Non-Hispanic Other"
    ))
  ) %>%
  arrange(Outcome, Predictor) %>%
  mutate(Predictor = as.character(Predictor))

##flextable

# Each outcome now has 5 rows: 1 main effect + 4 interaction rows

ft <- flextable(manuscript_df) %>%

  set_header_labels(
    Outcome   = "Biological Aging Clock",
    Predictor = "Predictor Variable",
    M1 = "Model 1", M2 = "Model 2", M3 = "Model 3",
    M4 = "Model 4", M5 = "Model 5"
  ) %>%

  align(j = c("M1","M2","M3","M4","M5"), align = "center", part = "all") %>%
  align(j = c("Outcome","Predictor"),          align = "left",   part = "all") %>%

  # Merge the Outcome label across its 5 rows
  merge_v(j = "Outcome") %>%
  valign(j = "Outcome", valign = "top") %>%

  font(fontname = "Times New Roman", part = "all") %>%
  fontsize(size = 11, part = "all") %>%
  bold(part = "header") %>%

  #borders
  border_remove() %>%
  hline_top(border    = fp_border(color = "black", width = 1.5), part = "header") %>%
  hline_bottom(border = fp_border(color = "black", width = 1.5), part = "header") %>%
  hline_bottom(border = fp_border(color = "black", width = 1.5), part = "body") %>%

  # Divider between the two clocks (after row 5)
  hline(i = 5, border = fp_border(color = "gray", width = 0.5), part = "body") %>%

  # M1–M5 have no interaction terms
  colformat_char(j = c("M1","M2","M3","M4","M5"), na_str = "") %>%

  valign(valign = "top", part = "body") %>%
  autofit() %>%
  set_caption(caption = "Table 5. Multi-Model Regressions for Perceived Cancer Risk Predicting Epigenetic Age Acceleration Including Race/Ethnicity Interaction Term.")

#formatting (condense)
ft <- ft %>%
  # Constrain model columns to a fixed narrow width
  width(j = c("M1","M2","M3","M4","M5"), width = 0.8) %>%
  # Constrain descriptor columns
  width(j = "Outcome",    width = 1.0) %>%
  width(j = "Predictor",  width = 1.4)

ft
#export to word
read_docx() %>% 
  body_add_flextable(ft) %>% 
  print(target = "Output/rafaela_all_interactions_regressions_table.docx")



```

```{r regression plot}

#PREDICTION FUNCTION
#Uses marginaleffects::predictions() to get predicted values
#other covariates are kept constant (mean)
library(marginaleffects)

get_preds <- function(model, outcome_label, model_label) {
  
  # Grid: cancerrisk_STD from -2 to 2, all 4 race/eth groups
  pred_grid <- expand.grid(
    cancerrisk_STD = seq(-2, 2, by = 1),
    race_eth = levels(df_mice_completed$race_eth)
  )
  
  preds <- predictions(model, newdata = datagrid(
    cancerrisk_STD = seq(-2, 2, by = 1),
    race_eth = levels(df_mice_completed$race_eth)
  )) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    mutate(
      Outcome = outcome_label,
      Model   = model_label
    )
  
  return(preds)
}

#predictions for model 4 and model 5
preds_all <- bind_rows(
  get_preds(m4_grim_i, "GrimAge2 Acceleration",      "Model 4"),
  get_preds(m5_grim_i, "GrimAge2 Acceleration",      "Model 5"),
  get_preds(m4_dune_i, "DunedinPACE Pace of Aging",  "Model 4"),
  get_preds(m5_dune_i, "DunedinPACE Pace of Aging",  "Model 5")
)

#assinging corresponding colors to race levels
race_colors <- c(
  "non-Hispanic White" = "#C00000",   # red
  "non-Hispanic Black" = "#4472C4",  # blue
  "Hispanic"           = "#70AD47",  # green
  "non-Hispanic Other" = "#ED7D31"  # orange
)

#function for making the plot
make_plot <- function(data, outcome, panel_label) {
  data %>%
    filter(Outcome == outcome) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = cancerrisk_STD, y = estimate, 
               color = race_eth, group = race_eth)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    facet_wrap(~ Model, ncol = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = race_colors, name = "Race/Ethnicity") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = -2:2) +
    labs(
      title = panel_label,
      x     = "Perceived Cancer Risk (z-score)",
      y     = "Predicted Outcome (z-score)"
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_family = "Arial") +
    theme(
      plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title    = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.text     = element_text(size = 9),
      strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
      axis.title      = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.text       = element_text(size = 9)
    )
}

#building plots and combining for comparison
p_grim <- make_plot(preds_all, "GrimAge2 Acceleration",     "(A) GrimAge2 Acceleration")
p_dune <- make_plot(preds_all, "DunedinPACE Pace of Aging", "(B) DunedinPACE Pace of Aging")

# Stack vertically with shared legend
final_plot <- p_grim / p_dune +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

final_plot

#Save plot as image (ggsave)
ggsave("interaction_plot_M4_M5.png", final_plot, 
       width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

#Embed in flextable
ft_plot <- flextable(data.frame(x = "")) %>%
  delete_part(part = "header") %>%
  compose(
    i = 1, j = 1,
    value = as_paragraph(
      as_image(src = "interaction_plot_M4_M5.png", width = 7, height = 7)
    )
  ) %>%
  border_remove() %>%
  set_caption(caption = "Figure16. Predicted Epigenetic Age Acceleration by Perceived Cancer Risk and Race/Ethnicity (Models 4 and 5).")

doc <- read_docx() %>%
  body_add_flextable(ft_plot)

print(doc, target = "Output/interaction_plot_M4_M5.docx")




```
